summary: Insta-snapshot current `skill_features`/`rule_features` output over real `.claude/` fixtures — the equivalence baseline the extraction-unification swap must hold byte-identical

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

new.description: Insta snapshots of `extract::skill_features` and `extract::rule_features` over real Claude Code fixtures that mirror the actual on-disk layout (a representative `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, the dogfood-shaped `.claude/rules/*.md` — never a layout invented for the test, per `.claude/rules/rust.md`). Snapshot each resulting `Features` (Debug) so a later entry can prove the generic composed path yields feature-for-feature identical output. The committed `.snap` files are the baseline the swap must not change.

tests.asserts: the current built-in extractors' `Features` output over real skill/rule fixtures is captured as committed insta snapshots — the fixed target every wave entry diffs against.

acceptance: committed `.snap` files pin `skill_features`/`rule_features` output over real fixtures; cargo test green with accepted snapshots.
notes: Opens the source-not-mechanism wave: pin the built-in extractors' output BEFORE any swap so every later entry diffs a fixed target (inbox ruling). BUILTIN-EXTRACT-GENERIC repoints this test to the generic path; its `.snap` must stay byte-identical — that unchanged snapshot IS the equivalence proof.

summary: Embed `kinds/skill/KIND.md` + `kinds/rule/KIND.md` via `build.rs` (beside the packages embed) and expose them as in-crate `CustomKind` definitions

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

new.description: The embedded built-in KIND std-lib, mirroring `src/builtin.rs` for packages: `include!` the generated `BUILTIN_KINDS` table and parse a named definition into a `crate::kind::CustomKind` via `CustomKind::from_header` over its `+++`-fenced `KIND.md` header. Accessors `definition(name)`/`definitions()`; `skill`/`rule` resolve from the embedded set with no on-disk config, validated as any kind is. The parse-into-CustomKind path already exists — this only sources the header from embedded product data instead of `.temper/kinds/`.

edit.description: Also walk the `kinds/` product tree (beside the existing `packages/` walk) and generate `BUILTIN_KINDS: &[(&str, &str)]` — `(name, KIND.md source)` per `kinds/<name>/KIND.md`, `include_str!`-embedded, sorted, with a `rerun-if-changed` per file. Auto-detected exactly like the packages embed: adding a built-in kind needs no edit here.
Add `pub mod builtin_kind;` beside `pub mod builtin;` (this entry owns the line).

tests.asserts: `definition("skill")`/`definition("rule")` parse the embedded `KIND.md` into a `CustomKind` whose composed `Extraction` and `governs` match the hand-authored definitions; an unknown name yields `None`.

acceptance: the two built-in `KIND.md` definitions ship embedded and resolve in-crate to `CustomKind` values with no on-disk config; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: `kinds/**` is human territory — build EMBEDS, never writes (chain.ts NOTE); both definitions are hand-authored (b06f3a0). Mirror the packages embed: `build.rs` emits `BUILTIN_KINDS`; `src/builtin_kind.rs` parses each via the existing `CustomKind::from_header`. No behavior swap yet — extraction still runs the old path until BUILTIN-EXTRACT-GENERIC.

summary: Drive built-in skill/rule extraction through the embedded KIND definitions and the generic composed path; retire the hand-coded `skill_features`/`rule_features`

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

edit.description: Add the adapter read face: turn a `Skill`/`Rule` IR into a `crate::kind::Unit` (frontmatter from the typed fields + `extra`, the byte-faithful body, provenance, `satisfies`, `published_requirements`) and run the embedded kind's `Extraction::extract` to yield `extract::Features`. This IR→Unit conversion is the engine-code adapter the source-not-mechanism direction leaves sanctioned; the per-field feature mapping moves into the composed `kinds/*/KIND.md` extraction.

Retire `skill_features` and `rule_features` (the hand-coded per-field mappings) and their `#[cfg(test)]` unit tests — symbol retirement as an EDIT, never a `retire` path (inbox fence correction). Keep the shared `pub(crate)` surface extractors (`body_line_count`/`body_headings`/`body_sections`/`source_dir_name`/`json_to_feature`) the composed path already reuses; the `Features`/`FeatureValue`/`Kind`/`Section` types stay.

Repoint the built-in feature call sites — `run_check` (~541–545) and the `Why` command (~405–408) — from `extract::skill_features`/`extract::rule_features` to the embedded skill/rule KIND extraction driver in `builtin_kind`. `assemble_by_kind`, the `engine::validate` streams, and coverage/graph tiers are otherwise unchanged (they range over `Features`).

Repoint the snapshots from `skill_features`/`rule_features` to the new embedded-extraction driver; the committed `.snap` files MUST remain byte-identical — the unchanged snapshot is the equivalence proof.

tests.asserts: the embedded-extraction driver produces `Features` byte-identical to the pinned `skill_features`/`rule_features` snapshots (the `.snap` files are unchanged).

acceptance: built-in skill/rule `check` features are computed by running the embedded `kinds/*/KIND.md` extraction over an IR-derived `Unit`; `skill_features`/`rule_features` are gone; the pinned equivalence snapshot is unchanged; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test and the self-check gate all green.
notes: Completes the wave. `skill_features`/`rule_features` (hand-coded field mapping) retire as an EDIT to src/extract.rs naming the symbols, never a `retire` path (inbox fence correction). Engine code stays only at adapter faces (Skill/Rule IR→Unit; parse/emit). Equivalence proven by the unchanged pin snapshot; blocked serially so no open entry shares these files.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; the surface-language chain it waited on has shipped, so the blocker is priority alone — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib by build.rs (the packages/ product tree — NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Add `agent_features` mirroring the built-in extraction driver (post-unification this is the embedded KIND path, not a hand-coded feature fn).
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred — more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; revive only if a story demands it. On revival, add the agent KIND definition under kinds/ (post-unification) and serialize its shared-file blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) via blockedBy against any then-open entry touching those files.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.

summary: Add CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md — the two missing launch community docs (two-sided AI-authorship disclosure; vuln-reporting evidence bar)

per.section: The community surface — small, honest, gated

gate.reason: build's writable fence (.flume/chain.ts BUILD_WRITABLE_PATHS) lists only README/CHANGELOG/AGENTS/LICENSE among root docs — no root CONTRIBUTING.md/SECURITY.md glob — so build would bail; a human must widen the fence (chain.ts is human territory) and enable private vulnerability reporting (a repo setting) before build can author these

new.description: Small and honest per the Decision: how to file a good issue, 'search first', PRs by prior discussion, and the two-sided AI-authorship policy (inbound AI-assisted contributions welcome WITH disclosure; the contributor must understand and defend the change without the assistant). Expectation-setting, not an invitation to co-maintain.

Vulnerability-reporting policy with an explicit evidence bar — a report must demonstrate, not speculate (the 'unverified AI-generated vuln reports are the new spam' lesson). Points at private vulnerability reporting (the GitHub repo setting a human enables).

acceptance: CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md exist at root, matching specs/55's launch-set Decisions (two-sided disclosure; demonstrate-not-speculate); the fence has been widened so build's entry-fence preflight admits them.
notes: Launch set (specs/55). Already on disk: README, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE-MIT/APACHE, issue forms (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,feature,config}.yml). Missing only CONTRIBUTING.md + SECURITY.md. Both are root-level per the spec but outside build's root-docs fence — parked on a human fence-widen (chain.ts) + enabling private reporting. Parked entries are skipped by the entry-fence gate, so this files cleanly.
